paper_title: Optically Stimulated Luminescence and Isothermal Thermoluminescence Dating Methods

methods.description: This method involves the collection and preparation of samples for optically stimulated luminescence (OSL) dating, which is used to determine the age of sediment samples by measuring the amount of luminescence emitted from mineral grains.

steps.description: Samples were collected in opaque PVC or aluminum tubes.
Samples were wet sieved to isolate the 180-250 or 150-180 µm grain sizes.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve samples to isolate specific grain sizes

steps.description: Samples of fluvial carbonates were crushed and etched with hydrochloric acid (HCl 10%) to dissociate individual silicate minerals before wet sieving.
steps.searchable_summary: crush and etch fluvial carbonates with HCl

steps.description: Quartz concentrates were prepared through treatment with hydrogen peroxide (H2O2 27%) and HCl 10% to eliminate organic matter and carbonates.
steps.searchable_summary: treat quartz with H2O2 and HCl to remove impurities

steps.description: HF 40% was used to dissolve the outer shell of quartz grains and remove other minerals.
steps.searchable_summary: use HF to dissolve quartz outer shell

steps.description: Remnant feldspar and heavy minerals were separated from quartz grains through heavy liquid separation using lithium metatungstate solution.
steps.searchable_summary: separate minerals using heavy liquid separation

steps.description: Luminescence measurements were carried out with Risø TL/OSL DA-20 readers.
steps.searchable_summary: perform luminescence measurements with Risø readers

steps.description: The SAR protocol was used for equivalent dose estimation.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate equivalent dose using SAR protocol

steps.description: Only aliquots or single grains with recycling ratios between 0.9 and 1.1 and recuperation less than 5% were used for equivalent dose calculation.
steps.searchable_summary: validate aliquots based on recycling ratios and recuperation

steps.description: Equivalent doses for age calculation were determined with the central age model (CAM) or minimum age model (MAM).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate equivalent doses using CAM or MAM

methods.description: This method involves the use of isothermal thermoluminescence (ITL) signals to perform dose recovery tests and determine the burial age of sediment samples.

steps.description: Quartz grains in the 180-250 µm grain size were isolated using standard procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate quartz grains of specific size

steps.description: OSL dating using a SAR protocol gave an equivalent dose of 0.31 ± 0.01 Gy.
steps.searchable_summary: perform OSL dating using SAR protocol

steps.description: A dating protocol relying on the ITL 270 o C and ITL 310 o C signals was tested for dose recovery tests.
steps.searchable_summary: test ITL signals for dose recovery

steps.description: Dose response curves were built using radiation doses up to 1200 Gy.
The net ITL 270 o C and ITL 310 o C signals were calculated by integrating over the first 5 s of the light emission curve.
